title: leretia angustifolia
intro: Leretia angustifolia, also known as Narrow-leafed Leretia, is a perennial herb native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is rich in essential oils and flavonoids, providing various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which can help protect against infections and oxidative stress. Leretia angustifolia is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can also be added fresh to salads or used as a garnish, making it a versatile and valuable herbal remedy.
